key_insight: Trajectory-aware flow matching injects physics-guided optimization history into conditional topology generation, enabling faster and more faithful designs than endpoint-only generative training.
engineering_relevance: The framework can speed up topology optimization workflows by producing multiple feasible candidate structures with fewer sampling steps and without costly inference-time optimization.
method: Linear FMTO: interpolate between Gaussian noise and a BESO reference topology.
Trajectory-aware FMTO: build the probability path and target velocity from volume-fraction-indexed BESO states.
Train a flow model to match the transport field for conditional topology generation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved compliance-related performance, volume-fraction satisfaction, and topology fidelity, with substantially fewer sampling steps than a diffusion baseline, including strong results under limited training data.
watch_item: Too much trajectory weighting can over-constrain transport and reduce generation stability, so the path design and anchor density need careful tuning.
paper_title: Trajectory-Aware Flow Matching for Topology Optimisation
